steps.title: Find the causal clue
steps.content:
The passage says Loris Taylor wanted stations like KABU to thrive, which explains why she took the next action.
This sets up a cause‑effect relationship that the transition must show.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the choices
steps.content:
The logical relationship is cause-effect. Taylor's desire for stations like KABU to thrive directly caused her to found Native Public Media.
  • Choice A — "Regardless,": signals that the second action happened despite something, implying an obstacle — no obstacle is mentioned, so fails. ✗
  • Choice B — "Besides,": signals addition of an unrelated point, not a result, so fails. ✗
  • Choice C — "Therefore,": signals that the founding of Native Public Media follows as a result of her goal, so fits. ✓
  • Choice D — "In other words,": signals restatement, but founding a new organization is not a restatement of wanting stations to thrive, so fails. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
